name: Engine COMP
description: The Engine COMP will run a .tox file (component) in a separate process. It uses TouchEngine to manage these processes and pass data between the loaded component and the main project.
summary: The Engine COMP will run a .tox file (component) in a separate process. It uses TouchEngine to manage these processes and pass data between the loaded component and the main project.

parameters.name: Tox File
parameters.label: Tox File
parameters.description: Parameter of this operator. See operator description for details.

parameters.name: Unload
parameters.label: Unload
parameters.description: unload - Unload the currently loaded component.

parameters.name: Reload
parameters.label: Reload
parameters.description: reload - Reload the currently loaded component.

parameters.name: Reload on Crash
parameters.label: Reload on Crash
parameters.description: reloadoncrash - If the TouchEngine instance quits for any reason, restart it.

parameters.name: Asset Paths
parameters.label: Asset Paths
parameters.description:
assetpaths -  - Specify how relative paths inside the component are resolved.

 project - Relative paths inside the component are relative to the project file running TouchEngine. comp - Relative paths inside the component are relative to the component ( file).

parameters.name: Relative to Project File (.toe)
parameters.label: Relative to Project File (.toe)
parameters.description: project - Relative paths inside the component are relative to the project file running TouchEngine. comp - Relative paths inside the component are relative to the component ( file).

parameters.name: Callbacks DAT
parameters.label: Callbacks DAT
parameters.description: callbacks - The Callbacks  will execute for events related to the TouchEngine instance.

parameters.name: Clock
parameters.label: Clock
parameters.description:
clock -  - Specify the temporal connection to the TouchEngine instance.

 synced - Time is strictly synchronized between the Engine  and the TouchEngine instance. independent - The TouchEngine instance runs according to its own internal clock.

parameters.name: Synchronized
parameters.label: Synchronized
parameters.description: synced - Time is strictly synchronized between the Engine  and the TouchEngine instance. independent - The TouchEngine instance runs according to its own internal clock.

parameters.name: Match Local Component Rate
parameters.label: Match Local Component Rate
parameters.description: matchrate - When on, the component will be cooked in TouchEngine at the same rate as the Engine .

parameters.name: Frames per Second
parameters.label: Frames per Second
parameters.description: fps - The framerate for cooking the component in TouchEngine.

parameters.name: Wait for Render
parameters.label: Wait for Render
parameters.description:
wait - When enabled, if a frame takes a long time to cook in TouchEngine the Engine  will wait during cooking rather than dropping the late frame.
This behaviour is affected by the size of the output buffer: eg if Out Buffer Frames is 4, the Engine  will wait for the 4th most recent frame to complete.

parameters.name: Render Timeout
parameters.label: Render Timeout
parameters.description: timeout - When waiting for a frame, TouchEngine will give up waiting after this many milliseconds.

parameters.name: In Buffer Auto
parameters.label: In Buffer Auto
parameters.description: inauto - Automatically manage the number of input frames queued.

parameters.name: In Buffer Frames
parameters.label: In Buffer Frames
parameters.description:
inframes - The number of input frames to queue before passing them to the TouchEngine instance.
To accommodate potential fluctuations in time-slice in the TouchEngine instance,  inputs must send a number of frames ahead of time.

parameters.name: Out Buffer Auto
parameters.label: Out Buffer Auto
parameters.description: outauto - Automatically manage the number of output frames queued.

parameters.name: Out Buffer Frames
parameters.label: Out Buffer Frames
parameters.description:
outframes - The number of output frames to queue after receiving them from the TouchEngine instance.
To accommodate potential fluctuations in time-slice in the Engine ,  outputs must send a number of frames ahead of time.

parameters.name: Pre-Roll
parameters.label: Pre-Roll
parameters.description: preroll - At initialization, run for this long before entering the ready state.

parameters.name: Pre-Roll Units
parameters.label: Pre-Roll Units
parameters.description:
prerollunits -  - The units in which the Pre-Roll is measured.

 frames - The Pre-Roll is measured in frames. seconds - The Pre-Roll is measured in seconds.

parameters.name: F
parameters.label: F
parameters.description: frames - The Pre-Roll is measured in frames. seconds - The Pre-Roll is measured in seconds.

parameters.name: Ready when
parameters.label: Ready when
parameters.description:
readywhen -  - Specify when the Engine  will go into the ready state.

 loaded - The Engine  will go into the ready state when TouchEngine has loaded the component. buffered - The Engine  will go into the ready state when TouchEngine has loaded the component and sufficient frames have been cooked to fill the input and output buffers. running - The Engine  will go into the ready state when TouchEngine has loaded the component, sufficient frames have been cooked to fill the input and output buffers and the component is running.

parameters.name: Component Loaded
parameters.label: Component Loaded
parameters.description: loaded - The Engine  will go into the ready state when TouchEngine has loaded the component. buffered - The Engine  will go into the ready state when TouchEngine has loaded the component and sufficient frames have been cooked to fill the input and output buffers. running - The Engine  will go into the ready state when TouchEngine has loaded the component, sufficient frames have been cooked to fill the input and output buffers and the component is running.

parameters.name: Start when Initialized
parameters.label: Start when Initialized
parameters.description: startoninit - When enabled, playback will start as soon as the component has initialized and is in the ready state.

parameters.name: Initialize
parameters.label: Initialize
parameters.description: initialize - Reload the  file, restarting the TouchEngine instance.

parameters.name: Start
parameters.label: Start
parameters.description: start - Starts playback of the component in the TouchEngine instance.

parameters.name: Play
parameters.label: Play
parameters.description: play - Turn cooking in the TouchEngine instance on or off.

parameters.name: Go to Done
parameters.label: Go to Done
parameters.description: gotodone - Puts the Engine  in the done state.

parameters.name: On Done
parameters.label: On Done
parameters.description:
ondone -  - Determines the actions taken, if any, when the Engine  enters the done state.

 donothing - Apart from the state change, nothing happens and TouchEngine continues to run the component. pause - Playback of the loaded component is paused. unload - The loaded component is unloaded. reinit - The loaded component is unloaded and then initialization takes place. restart - The component remains loaded and playback begins again. quit - TouchEngine is completely shut down.

parameters.name: Do Nothing
parameters.label: Do Nothing
parameters.description: donothing - Apart from the state change, nothing happens and TouchEngine continues to run the component. pause - Playback of the loaded component is paused. unload - The loaded component is unloaded. reinit - The loaded component is unloaded and then initialization takes place. restart - The component remains loaded and playback begins again. quit - TouchEngine is completely shut down.

parameters.name: On Engine COMP Create
parameters.label: On Engine COMP Create
parameters.description:
oncompcreate -  - Specify what happens when the Engine  is created, for example when the project is loaded.

 nothing - Nothing happens when the Engine  is created. launch - TouchEngine is started when the Engine  is created. Any specified component is not loaded. initialize - TouchEngine is started and any specified component is loaded and the Engine  is initialized and put into the ready state.

parameters.name: Launch Engine Process
parameters.label: Launch Engine Process
parameters.description: launch - Starts the TouchEngine process if it is not running.

parameters.name: Quit Engine Process
parameters.label: Quit Engine Process
parameters.description: quit - Stops the TouchEngine process if it is running.

parameters.name: Extension
parameters.label: Extension
parameters.description:
ext - Sequence of info for creating extensions on this component



  ext0object - A number of class instances that can be attached to the component.


    
  ext0name - Optional name to search by, instead of the instance class name.



  ext0promote - Controls whether or not the extensions are visible directly at the component level, or must be accessed through the .ext member.  Example:  n.Somefunction vs n.ext.Somefunction

parameters.name: Object
parameters.label: Object
parameters.description: ext0object - A number of class instances that can be attached to the component.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.label: Name
parameters.description: ext0name - Optional name to search by, instead of the instance class name.

parameters.name: Promote
parameters.label: Promote
parameters.description: ext0promote - Controls whether or not the extensions are visible directly at the component level, or must be accessed through the .ext member.  Example:  n.Somefunction vs n.ext.Somefunction

parameters.name: Re-Init Extensions
parameters.label: Re-Init Extensions
parameters.description: reinitextensions - Recompile all extension objects. Normally extension objects are compiled only when they are referenced and their definitions have changed.

parameters.name: Parent Shortcut
parameters.label: Parent Shortcut
parameters.description: parentshortcut - Specifies a name you can use anywhere inside the component as the path to that component. See Parent Shortcut.

parameters.name: Global OP Shortcut
parameters.label: Global OP Shortcut
parameters.description: opshortcut - Specifies a name you can use anywhere at all as the path to that component. See Global OP Shortcut.

parameters.name: Internal OP
parameters.label: Internal OP
parameters.description:
iop - Sequence header for internal operators.



  iop0shortcut - Specifies a name you can use anywhere inside the component as a path to "Internal " below. See Internal Operators.



  iop0op - The path to the Internal OP inside this component. See Internal Operators.

parameters.name: Shortcut
parameters.label: Shortcut
parameters.description: iop0shortcut - Specifies a name you can use anywhere inside the component as a path to "Internal " below. See Internal Operators.

parameters.name: OP
parameters.label: OP
parameters.description: iop0op - The path to the Internal OP inside this component. See Internal Operators.

parameters.name: Operator Viewer
parameters.label: Operator Viewer
parameters.description: opviewer - Select which operator's node viewer to use when the Node View parameter above is set to Operator .

parameters.name: Enable Cloning
parameters.label: Enable Cloning
parameters.description: enablecloning - Control if the OP should be actively cloneing. Turning this off causes this node to stop cloning it's ' Master'.

parameters.name: Enable Cloning Pulse
parameters.label: Enable Cloning Pulse
parameters.description: enablecloningpulse - Instantaneously clone the contents.

parameters.name: Clone Master
parameters.label: Clone Master
parameters.description: clone -  to a component used as the Master Clone.

parameters.name: Load on Demand
parameters.label: Load on Demand
parameters.description: loadondemand - Loads the component into memory only when required. Good to use for components that are not always used in the project.

parameters.name: Enable External .tox
parameters.label: Enable External .tox
parameters.description: enableexternaltox - When on (default), the external  file will be loaded when the  starts and the contents of the  will match that of the external . This can be turned off to avoid loading from the referenced external  on startup if desired (the contents of the  are instead loaded from the  file). Useful if you wish to have a  reference an external  but not always load from it unless you specifically push the Re-Init Network parameter button.

parameters.name: Enable External .tox Pulse
parameters.label: Enable External .tox Pulse
parameters.description: enableexternaltoxpulse - This button will re-load from the external  file (if present).

parameters.name: External .tox Path
parameters.label: External .tox Path
parameters.description: externaltox -  to a  file on disk which will source the component's contents upon start of a . This allows for components to contain networks that can be updated independently. If the  file can not be found, whatever the  file was saved with will be loaded.

parameters.name: Reload Custom Parameters
parameters.label: Reload Custom Parameters
parameters.description: reloadcustom - When this checkbox is enabled, the values of the component's Custom Parameters are reloaded when the .tox is reloaded. This only affects top-level parameters on the component, all parameters on nodes inside the component are always reloaded with the .tox.

parameters.name: Reload Built-In Parameters
parameters.label: Reload Built-In Parameters
parameters.description: reloadbuiltin - When this checkbox is enabled, the values of the component's built-in parameters are reloaded when the .tox is reloaded. This only affects top-level parameters on the component, all parameters on nodes inside the component are always reloaded with the .tox.

parameters.name: Save Backup of External
parameters.label: Save Backup of External
parameters.description: savebackup - When this checkbox is enabled, a backup copy of the component specified by the External  parameter is saved in the  file.  This backup copy will be used if the External  can not be found. This may happen if the  was renamed, deleted, or the  file is running on another computer that is missing component media.

parameters.name: Sub-Component to Load
parameters.label: Sub-Component to Load
parameters.description: subcompname - When loading from an External  file, this option allows you to reach into the  and pull out a  and make that the top-level , ignoring everything else in the file (except for the contents of that ). For example if a  file named project1 contains project1/geo1, putting geo1 as the Sub- to Load, will result in geo1 being loaded in place of the current . If this parameter is blank, it just loads the  file normally using the top level  in the file.

parameters.name: Relative File Path Behavior
parameters.label: Relative File Path Behavior
parameters.description:
relpath -  - Set whether the child file paths within this  are relative to the  itself or the , or inherit from parent.

 inherit - Inherit setting from parent. project - The path, when specified as a relative path, will be relative to the  file. externaltox - The path, when specified as a relative path, will be relative to the  file. When no external  file is specified, or when Enable External  is not toggled on, this doesn't have any impact.

parameters.name: Use Parent's Behavior
parameters.label: Use Parent's Behavior
parameters.description: inherit - Inherit setting from parent. project - The path, when specified as a relative path, will be relative to the  file. externaltox - The path, when specified as a relative path, will be relative to the  file. When no external  file is specified, or when Enable External  is not toggled on, this doesn't have any impact.
